inputs: Most grapplers don't suck, but some do!  And one of them is...

movesList.name: ub
inputs: u  uf      Jump Up-Back            Jump Up         Jump Up-Forward

movesList.name: b -- n -- f
inputs: Walk Back / Block       Neutral         Walk Forward

movesList.name: db
inputs: d  df      Crouch / Low Block      Crouch          Offensive Crouch

movesList.name: LP
inputs: MP  HP      Light Punch         Medium Punch        Hard Punch

movesList.name: LK
inputs: MK  HK      Light Kick          Medium Kick         Hard Kick

movesList.name: xzvB
inputs: -  The attack is interruptable on both hits.

movesList.name: SC
inputs: -  Super Combo
...Neutral Breakfall      Press PP when knocked into the air
...Front Breakfall        Press f + PP when knocked into the air
...Back Breakfall         Press b + PP when knocked into the air

movesList.name: Throw
inputs: b / f + PP / KK when close

movesList.name: Air Throw
inputs: Any dir. but d / u + PP when close in air
when blocking special moves, super combos, and their ilk.
air-block projectiles, any attack your opponent does in mid-air (or
an attack that takes them off the ground, like Ryu or Ken's
Hurricane Kick).  You can't block attacks performed on the ground,
normal block (standing / crouching / mid-air), but you don't lose
any power from your GP gauge for the _first block_.  So if you're
hit by a multi-hit move, as long as you remain blocking,
successive hits drain the normal amount of GP energy from your
gauge.  You'll know if you pulled off a GP block since you'll
of your OC gauge (in V-ISM).  This is in addition to losing 1 block
